what do a company does, how to read a report, 
then buy and read Benjamin Graham's intelligent investor book.
Learn whats the difference between a speculant and an investor... 

and start with stocks maybe on paper account. 
if it is okay, you can start to check what is a derivative asset, and whats the purpose behind it, how the fair value is determined etc. 
so yepp

the key is to know what is the vanna convexity and what territory is beyond the zero vanna: positive or negative.
if positive, you need relatively high vol to break through the zero vanna,

and also it is totally contradictionary what the speed profile would indicate, and this is why we have to watch the "hidden" effects too, that i described this morning

comment.body:
have you read Benjamin Graham's works? 
edge is buying assets at prices significantly below intrinsic value, and the way you manage the risk. this is crucial part: no overhedge or underhedge.. delta–gamma VaR (i will cover in the future, Expected Shortfall etc.) 
also, you need to use math like graham number, or margin of safety, that is basically: intrinsic value minus market value divided by the intrinsic value. it help you to protect against estimation errors and market volatility.. 
And the Graham number is the square root of 22.5 * EPS * BVPS (book value per share)... and if a stock is trading below this number, it is potentially undervalued. 

The hedging part is the real challenge.. and thats where hedge funds do mistakes. thats how i predicted this upward movement, bcs they were overexposed to long vol hedges, and they had to cover their losses that those hedging positions caused... its not easy at all to estimate the distribution
